**Job Purpose**

The International Regulatory Affairs Project Manager is responsible for collaborating with European and global Health Authorities to secure new product registrations and ensure the ongoing maintenance of existing marketing authorizations. This role acts as a critical link between the company, its subsidiaries, and international partners to ensure compliance with global regulatory standards, particularly within the rare disease and niche market sectors**Key Responsibilities**
**Regulatory Strategy \& Dossier Management**
* Lead the preparation, submission, and strategic follow\-up of all regulatory filings, including pre\-authorization dossiers (ODDs, IMPD, IND, PIP, NDA, MAA) and post\-authorization lifecycle management (variations, renewals, annual reports, and re\-assessments).
* Provide high\-level scientific documentation and regulatory support to international subsidiaries and external partners.
* Monitor and interpret international legislation to ensure the company’s portfolio remains compliant with evolving global standards.

**Operational Execution \& Compliance**
* Oversee and track the progress of all global filings, ensuring timely responses to queries from Health Authorities.
* Review and approve mock\-ups for labeling and final packaging in coordination with the production department and partners.
* Manage and supervise regulatory work outsourced to Contract Research Organizations (CROs) and external experts.

**Communication \& Stakeholder Management**
* Act as a primary point of contact for internal departments, global subsidiaries, and external stakeholders (Health Authorities, Manufacturers, and Professional Bodies like LEEM, AFAR, DIA).
* Facilitate internal knowledge sharing regarding regulatory issues to maintain a high level of expertise across the team.
* Represent the company at external meetings and provide detailed reporting to senior management.
**Required Education**

University Degree in Pharmacy (Pharmacist) or a related Scientific field.**Required Skills and Experience**
* 4 to 5 years of experience in the Pharmaceutical Industry, with a proven track record specifically in Regulatory Affairs.
* Prior experience in the Rare Disease field or Niche Markets is highly preferred.
* Proven experience in handling both pre\-approval (IND/IMPD) and post\-approval (variations/renewals) dossiers.
* Strong ability to draft, review, and edit complex scientific and regulatory submission files.
**Required Behaviours and Competencies**
* Ability to build strong relationships in a multi\-cultural environment and communicate effectively with diverse stakeholders.
* A flexible and proactive "hands\-on" approach to identifying issues and implementing effective solutions.
* Strong capacity to prioritize tasks, manage deadlines, and maintain financial awareness of project scopes.
* A natural team player who promotes a good level of understanding and interest in regulatory issues among colleagues.
* Ability to act with autonomy while keeping management and partners appropriately informed.

Native or professional fluency in English (written and spoken).

title: Test Engineer
description:
**Location:**Saronno, IT
**Test Engineer – Healthcare (Surgical)**
 **Saronno, Italy**

Barco Healthcare is looking for a hands‑on Test Engineer to join our R\&D team and support the development and industrialisation of next‑generation surgical products.  

In this role, you will work in an international, multidisciplinary R\&D environment, playing a key part in ensuring product quality, safety, and regulatory compliance throughout the product development lifecycle.

**Your Role**
As a Test Engineer, you will support **New Product Introduction (NPI)** projects by planning, executing, and coordinating verification and validation activities. You will work closely with engineering, industrialisation, quality, and external partners to ensure our products meet **international medical device standards** and Barco’s high‑quality expectations.

**Key Responsibilities**
* Analyse design inputs, project specifications, and regulatory requirements
* Define and execute test plans in collaboration with NPI project managers
* Perform verification and validation testing across different development stages
* Support and execute:
	+ **Environmental testing** (climatic, vibration)
	+ **EMC testing**
	+ **Electrical safety testing**
* Coordinate testing activities with external laboratories and notified bodies
* Conduct gap and conformity analysis against applicable international standards
* Support industrialisation activities, including zero‑series and pre‑series builds
* Contribute to the continuous improvement of testing processes, documentation, and tools
* Collaborate effectively with local teams and Barco headquarters


**Your Profile**
* Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electronics, or equivalent experience
* Hands‑on experience in a laboratory or test environment
* Exposure to testing, validation, or certification activities
* Ability to work independently, organise work, and take initiative
* Strong communication skills and a structured, analytical mindset
* Fluent in Italian and English

  


**Strong Plus**
* Experience in the **medical device / healthcare industry**
* Familiarity with **EMC, environmental, and electrical safety testing**
* Knowledge of medical device standards (e.g. **IEC 60601 series**)
* Background in testing laboratories, notified bodies, or other regulated industries

  


**What We Offer**
* A role in innovative healthcare technology that directly impacts patient care
* Exposure to cutting‑edge surgical products
* An international working environment with strong cross‑functional collaboration
* Opportunities for learning, development, and internal growth
* Minimal travel requirements

  


️ We are committed to conducting our business activities with the highest standards of integrity, responsibility and compliance across all aspects of our operations. This includes adherence to applicable laws, regulations and internal policies related to ethical conduct, quality standards, cyber security, sustainability, data protection \& confidentiality and safety.


**D\&I Statement**  

At Barco, innovation drives everything we do. We believe that diversity fuels creativity, bringing us closer to our colleagues and customers. Inclusion and equity aren't just values—they're core capabilities that propel us toward our shared goals and mission.

title: AI - Grants & Collaboration Specialist
description:
Località:
Pieve Emanuele, Milano, IT, 20072  

We are looking for a Junior Data Scientist to join the Humanitas AI Center, a multidisciplinary research unit dedicated to the development and application of Artificial Intelligence in healthcare.



The selected candidate will contribute to applied clinical AI research projects focused on the analysis of structured and tabular clinical data, with the goal of developing patient stratification models, prognostic and predictive models, and clinical decision\-support tools. Example use cases include patient selection for advanced therapies and data\-driven strategies to safely optimize treatment duration.



In addition to tabular data modeling, the role offers exposure to Deep Learning techniques and multimodal projects, potentially involving medical images and unstructured clinical text, in collaboration with other teams within the Humanitas AI Center.



You will work closely with senior data scientists, clinicians, and researchers, contributing to the full data science pipeline: from data exploration and feature engineering to model development, validation, and interpretation.


**About the Humanitas AI Center**



The Humanitas AI Center focuses on research and development of AI\-based predictive and decision\-support systems to improve clinical care and optimize healthcare processes. Our activities span multiple domains, including clinical data modeling, natural language processing on electronic health records, and medical imaging.



We are a multidisciplinary team of data scientists, engineers, clinicians, and researchers working closely with hospital departments to translate AI research into real clinical impact.

  


**Responsibilities**


* Support the development of machine learning and deep learning models for clinical prediction, prognosis, and patient stratification
* Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) on clinical datasets and contribute to data cleaning and preprocessing
* Implement and evaluate ML and DL models using structured/tabular data, and potentially images or unstructured text
* Contribute to the use and extension of internal ML frameworks (e.g. MOSAIC)
* Collaborate with clinicians to translate clinical questions into data\-driven solutions
* Produce clear data visualizations, reports, and presentations for both technical and clinical stakeholders
* Support documentation, reproducibility, and good software practices in research projects

  


**Skills and Qualifications**


* Fluent in written and spoken English (Italian is a plus);
* Master’s degree (PhD would be a plus) in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Biomedical Engineering, Bioinformatics, or related STEM disciplines
* Basic to intermediate knowledge of machine learning techniques (e.g. regression models, tree\-based methods, ensemble models)
* Solid foundations in statistics (e.g. regression, hypothesis testing, model evaluation metrics)
* Proficiency in Python for data analysis and machine learning
* Experience with common data science libraries: pandas, numpy, scikit\-learn, scipy
* Familiarity with Deep Learning concepts and frameworks such as PyTorch and/or TensorFlow
* Ability to work with structured clinical datasets

  


**Nice to Have**


* Experience with Deep Learning projects involving images (e.g. medical imaging) or unstructured text (e.g. clinical notes, reports)
* Exposure to natural language processing (NLP) or computer vision methods
* Familiarity with survival analysis, time\-to\-event modeling, or longitudinal data
* Experience with version control systems (e.g. Git)
* Basic experience with cloud computing platforms (e.g. AWS, GCP, Azure)
* Familiarity with containerisation technologies (e.g. Docker)
* Basic knowledge of SQL or working with relational databases
* Prior experience with healthcare or clinical data

  


**Soft Skills**


* Strong interest in AI applied to healthcare and clinical research
* Curiosity and willingness to learn from senior colleagues and clinicians
* Ability to work effectively in multidisciplinary teams
* Attention to detail and commitment to data quality and reproducibility
* Good communication skills, especially when explaining results to non\-technical stakeholders


**Why you should consider this opportunity**
============================================



Humanitas Research Hospital is investing in data driven research and development of clinical support tools based on AI, you will contribute to the design and application of breakthrough technologies to be deployed in advanced clinical institutions.


You will get access to an extraordinary group of talented and passionate people coming from fields ranging from clinical sciences and healthcare management to informatics, bioinformatics and engineering, including our extended international research network.  


**Contract and duration**



We can offer a fixed\-term contract. Contract duration, salary, as well as employment level, will be defined based on candidate's profile.

  


*All candidate data collected from the application shall be processed in accordance with applicable law: Dlgs 198/2006 e dei Dlgs 215/2003 e 216/2003; privacy ex artt. 13 e 14 del Reg. UE 2016/679\.*

title: CYS - GCAP Joint Venture Headquarters - Delivery Manager - MBSE
description:
**Job Description:**


Leonardo is a global industrial group, among the main global players in Aerospace, Defence and Security that realises multi\-domain technological capabilities in Helicopters, Aircraft, Aerostructures, Electronics, Cyber Security and Space. With over 60,000 employees worldwide, the company has a solid industrial presence in Italy, the UK, Poland and the US. It also operates in 150 countries through subsidiaries, joint ventures and investments. A key player in major international strategic programmes, it is a technological and industrial partner of governments, defence administrations, institutions and companies.

**Within the GCAP Joint Venture, in the GCAP Technical Leadership UO, we are looking for a Delivery Manager – MBSE for GCAP HQ in Reading (UK).**


This role reports to the Product Owner \- Systems Engineering and is accountable for delivering and continuously improving the digital engineering capabilities that support the MBSE pillar. The focus is on enabling the toolchains, digital thread, and lifecycle environments that underpin model\-based systems engineering across the enterprise, ensuring authoritative models replace traditional Office documents or 2D CAD as the primary means of design, communication, and collaboration.


The Product Delivery Manager defines and brings into service the reference architectures and technical baselines for MBSE digital solutions (including Cameo System Modeler, SysML/UML modeling languages, integration with requirements management tool IBM DOORS NG and TeamCenter PLM, configuration and change control, test data management, and evidence capture). The role translates business and engineering needs into a clear capability roadmap and prioritized backlog, and ensures environments deliver end\-to\-end traceability from requirements through models, analyses, and validation, interoperable with enterprise platforms and adjacent pillars.


The role liaises closely with other DE\&D Product Owners and Delivery Managers and with engineering stakeholders to align on standards, interfaces, and adoption, while coordinating boundaries with process and integration owners. Through rigorous backlog management, vendor engagement, and technology evaluations, the Product Delivery Manager ensures the MBSE digital capability delivers the right enabling solutions at the right time in the right way.

**Key Capability Accountabilities and Objectives**

**Product Delivery and Lifecycle Management**

* Own the delivery and continuous improvement of **MBSE digital capabilities**, including toolchains for **SysML/UML modeling**, **requirements management (IBM DOORS NG)**, model repositories, configuration/change control, product line/variant management, and **digital thread connectivity**.
* Ensure **full integration with the DE Digital Thread**, guaranteeing **end‑to‑end traceability** between requirements, models (structure, behavior, interfaces, allocations), analyses, simulation results, verification activities, and test evidence.
* Translate business and engineering needs into **clear product roadmaps**, delivery plans, and **prioritized backlogs** for MBSE capabilities, aligned to program increments and enterprise priorities.
* Establish and promote **best practices** for model‑based engineering: requirement flowdown and allocation, architecture definition, behavior modeling (state/sequence/activity), interface and Interface Control Document management, parametric modeling and constraints, simulation‑based verification, and evidence generation.
* Define and deploy **technical baselines**, **methodologies**, and **work instructions** to enable consistent MBSE ways of working across programs and partners, including integration points with adjacent domains (software/hardware/safety/IVVQ).
* Drive adoption of **models as the authoritative source** across the lifecycle—replacing document‑centric practices (Office files, 2D CAD drawings) for planning, reviews, and cross‑discipline communication.
* Ensure solutions are delivered **on time**, within scope, and aligned with **quality, performance, security, and compliance** targets (including applicable regulatory, safety, and certification expectations).
* Manage upgrades, enhancements, and decommissioning across the digital product lifecycle with **minimal disruption** to dependent engineering teams and governed data migration plans.

**Stakeholder and Business Engagement**

* Capture, challenge, and consolidate **capability requirements** for MBSE to avoid duplication and fragmentation across teams, suppliers, and partner organizations.
* Communicate **product vision**, scope, risks, and progress clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences; maintain transparent roadmaps and release notes.
* Develop and implement **workforce and cultural initiatives** to embed model‑centric behaviors (authoritative models, model reviews, model health metrics), including training materials, onboarding paths, and role‑based enablement.
* Define and design **Domain and Supply Chain access models** and onboarding to MBSE repositories, ensuring appropriate **role‑based access**, data protection, and supplier collaboration patterns.
* Plan and support **business change** for transitioning people and processes to MBSE, including adoption milestones, competency frameworks, coaching, and community of practice facilitation.

**Governance, Standards and Data Quality**

* Ensure adherence to enterprise standards for **MBSE reference architectures**, environment baselines, **configuration \& variant management**, and digital thread enablement.
* Define and enforce **data models**, taxonomies, naming conventions, **metadata standards**, and access controls for SysML/UML models, requirements repositories, simulations, test artefacts, and compliance evidence.
* Establish **model governance** (reviews, sign‑off criteria, health checks, style guides, profiles/stereotypes), **baselining**, and **branching strategies** to maintain digital continuity and auditability.
* Support **regulatory, quality, and audit** requirements related to engineering data, traceability, and evidence pathways (e.g., safety cases, assurance cases, qualification/certification submissions).
* **Delivery Execution and Operational Excellence**
* Coordinate delivery across internal teams and suppliers, ensuring **clear ownership**, accountability, and effective **hand‑offs** within MBSE scope.
* Monitor **service performance**, capacity, availability, **technical debt**, risks, and dependencies across modeling repositories, integration bridges, and verification pipelines.
* Drive incident resolution, root cause analysis, and **continuous service improvement** for MBSE capabilities; implement observability and SLOs for critical services.
* Balance short‑term delivery pressures with **long‑term architectural strategy**, maintainability, scalability, and robust **data stewardship**; guard against tool sprawl and redundant integrations.

* **Vendor and Technology Management**
* Support relationship management with key technology vendors and system integrators for **MBSE platforms**, **IBM DOORS NG**, configuration management, and simulation orchestration tools.
* Support **contract management**, license optimization, and vendor performance reviews to ensure cost‑effective and compliant operation across programs.
* Contribute to **technology evaluations**, proofs of concept, and strategic tool selection to maintain an aligned, interoperable, and **future‑ready MBSE capability**.
* Drive technology innovation in **automation**, model verification, simulation integration/co‑simulation, digital twin alignment, and **evidence generation** to improve speed, quality, and assurance.

**Essential Qualifications**

* Degree in **Systems Engineering**, Engineering (Mechanical/Electrical/Aerospace), Computer Science, or related technical discipline.

* Demonstrated ability to manage **multi‑partner and supplier integration** within complex programmes and distributed teams.
* Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence and align diverse teams toward common objectives.
* Strong financial and resource management skills, including planning, budgeting, and cost control for digital engineering capabilities.

title: Senior AI Engineer (f/m/d)
description:
**About the AI Platform Team**

The AI Platform Team enables Awin teams to build, ship, and operate AI\-powered products safely, consistently, and efficiently.



We focus on the shared platform and enablement layer for AI development across the company. That includes reusable services, internal APIs and SDKs, platform patterns, observability, evaluation support, guardrails, cost visibility, and practical guidance that helps teams move from experimentation to production with confidence.



This is a team for engineers who enjoy building internal platforms, creating paved roads for others, and raising the engineering quality bar for AI development across the organization.


**About the role**



We are looking for a **Senior AI Platform Engineer \- Security \& Governance** to join the AI Platform Team and help define and implement the guardrails, privacy patterns, governance mechanisms, and safe defaults that allow AI adoption to scale responsibly across Awin.



In this role, you will work on the safety and control layer of AI development: AI security patterns, privacy and PII handling guidance, provider and model governance, reusable guardrails, and lightweight readiness mechanisms that improve safety without creating unnecessary process.



This role is best suited to someone who combines strong engineering judgment with real practical experience in security, governance, privacy, or compliance for AI\-powered systems. We care more about evidence of sound decision\-making and pragmatic implementation than about titles or years in a specific niche.


**What youʼll do**

* Define and improve practical guardrails and safe defaults for AI\-powered systems
* Help shape Awinʼs approach to privacy, PII handling, and data\-safe patterns for AI workflows
* Contribute to approved provider and model guidance, including practical validation and usage expectations
* Work with engineers to translate policy, risk, and governance needs into reusable platform patterns and engineering controls
* Help define production\-ready expectations for AI systems from a safety, governance, and risk perspective
* Contribute to readiness checks, automated validations, and scalable governance mechanisms that reduce reliance on manual approval processes
* Help improve logging, tracing, and observability practices so AI systems are auditable and safer to operate
* Partner closely with Security, Legal, Architecture, and product engineering teams to ensure standards are practical and proportionate
* Support teams in understanding AI\-specific risks such as unsafe outputs, provider misuse, privacy exposure, and poor trace hygiene
* Write clear documentation, patterns, and guidance that help teams apply standards consistently
* Contribute to strong engineering practices across the team through clean code, testing, and collaboration
* Mentor others and help raise the teamʼs overall maturity in AI safety, governance, and privacy\-aware engineering


**Requirements**

* Strong software engineering fundamentals and experience working on production systems
* Practical experience in security, governance, privacy, compliance, or risk\-related engineering work
* Clear hands\-on experience applying those controls to AI\- or LLM\-powered systems
* Strong understanding of AI\-specific risks, including privacy exposure, PII handling, unsafe outputs, provider/model risk, and misuse or abuse patterns
* Experience translating policy or risk requirements into practical engineering controls, patterns, or defaults
* Good understanding of how to make AI systems observable, auditable, and safer to operate in production
* Ability to work effectively with engineers, architects, and non\-engineering stakeholders such as Security or Legal
* Good practical experience with Python and/or JavaScript / TypeScript
* Comfortable working across the lifecycle of a platform capability, from design and implementation to rollout and iteration
* Good understanding of information security and how to design solutions with security in mind
* Comfortable applying unit testing, continuous integration, and continuous deploymentStrong communication skills, both synchronous and asynchronous
* Comfortable working through ambiguity and helping shape standards where the right balance between safety and speed is still evolving
* Strong judgment and pragmatism, with an ability to improve safety without introducing excessive bureaucracy


**Nice to have**


* Experience designing or implementing guardrails for AI or LLM\-powered systems
* Experience with AI governance automation, readiness gates, policy checks, or preflight validation patterns
* Familiarity with tracing, auditability, and logging standards for AI systems
* Familiarity with evaluation workflows, regression checks, and production readiness practices for AI
* Experience assessing third\-party AI providers or model usage from a risk or governance perspective
* Experience with privacy\-by\-design patterns in data\-sensitive systems
* Experience working in regulated or higher\-risk environments
* Experience writing clear developer guidance and turning repeated risk questions into reusable standards


**Tools and technologies you may work with:**


* Python
* JavaScript / TypeScript
* Azure / AWS
* LLM APIs
* CI/CD tooling
* Observability and tracing tools
* Policy and validation patterns
* Internal platform services, standards, and governance tooling


**Working style**

We are looking for someone who enjoys building in a space that is evolving quickly, but who stays grounded in pragmatic engineering and long\-term platform value.



A strong fit for this role is someone who:


* Thinks clearly about real\-world AI risk, not just theoretical concerns
* Can balance safety, speed, and developer experience
* Communicates clearly and helps others understand trade\-offs
* Prefers reusable controls and automation over manual gatekeeping
* Is comfortable collaborating across engineering, security, architecture, and legal boundaries
* Writes clear, actionable guidance rather than vague policy language
* Is proactive about identifying risk patterns early and turning them into scalable solutions
* Enjoys helping other engineers build safer systems without slowing them down unnecessarily


**Our Offer**


* **Flexi\-Week and Work\-Life Balance:** We prioritise your mental health and wellbeing, offering you a flexible four\-day Flexi\-Week at full pay and with no reduction to your annual holiday allowance. We also offer a variety of different paid special leaves.
* **Remote Working Allowance:** You will receive a monthly allowance to cover part of your running costs. In addition, we will support you in setting up your remote workspace appropriately.
* **Flexi\-Office:** We offer an international culture and flexibility through our Flexi\-Office and hybrid/remote work possibilities to work across Awin regions
* **Meal Vouchers:** You will be supported with a certain net sum to spend it on a variety of lunches.
* **Health \& Wellbeing:** The insurance covers several types of health, vision and / or dental treatments for you and for up to one additional family member.
* **Remote Working Furniture Package:** After 3 months of employment, you will be eligible for a furniture package, which should enable you to set up a proper workplace at your remote working location
* **Appreciation**: Thank and reward colleagues by sending them a voucher through our peer\-to\-peer program.


Established in 2000, Awin is proud of our dynamic, social and inclusive culture.



Like all businesses, we've had to adapt and nurture our culture in a virtual environment. Our virtual 'Life @ Awin' hub brings our colleagues from across the globe together for various social activities.



Diversity \& Inclusion are paramount to us, and we proudly pursue and hire diverse team members. We champion uniqueness and authenticity; this is who we are at our core. Our network of affiliate partnerships are diverse and transparent, as are the employees powering our vision to build the world's leading open partner ecosystem. We welcome all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you need support at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.



Awin is part of the Axel Springer group. Learn more at axelspringer.com/en/, and explore the Axel Springer Essentials here: axelspringer.com/en/inside/the\-essentials\-what\-we\-have\-adapted\-and\-why



Apply now to begin the next stage of your career at a progressive company that supports both your professional and personal development.

title: Field Service Engineer, Northern Italy Region
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Laboratory Setting, Some degree of PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) required (safety glasses, gowning, gloves, lab coat, ear plugs etc.), Will work with hazardous/toxic materials**Job Description**


Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. is the world leader in serving science, with annual revenue of $40 billion. Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Whether our customers are accelerating life sciences research, solving complex analytical challenges, improving patient diagnostics and therapies or increasing productivity in their laboratories, we are here to support them.

  

The Binding Site part of Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. seeks a **Field Service Engineer for North Italy** to provide top\-notch service and support for our products. This position is outstanding, enabling you to boost patient outcomes in multiple myeloma through your technical expertise and dedication providing specialized service and support on the installation, integration, servicing, repair, planned maintenance and troubleshooting leveraging product knowledge and technical competency

**A day in the life:**

* Be part of a dynamic and skilled team that enables our customers to successfully complete their important clinical work on the Binding Site part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, products portfolio.
* The Filed Service Engineer will ensure successful installation, system integration, validation, scheduled maintenance, upgrades, troubleshooting and repair of the systems and associated software.
* The role also involves provision of timely technical support to customers and colleagues including remote access support and cross\-functional technical investigation work \- efficiently diagnosing faults and restoring full system functionality.
* As an expert in their field, the Filed Service Engineer must be confident in participating in technical discussions on the product – both hardware and software.
* Perform scheduled preventative maintenance (PM) visits within the timeframes required.
* Complete service reports in Service CRM and ensure they are completed both fully and accurately.
* Maintain accurate records of spare parts, ensuring parts are used appropriately and support with spare part stock checks and reconciliation.
* Update instruments placed at customer laboratories with software and data management upgrades as required.
* Provide feedback to global product teams on instrument reliability and quality.
* Provide training courses of installation and maintenance to end\-user
* Make preliminary visits to laboratories to agree on positioning of the instrumentation, checking correct installation site and the need for any hydraulic/electric/logistic work.
* Support for interfacing LIS with the main company management tools
* Support and sponsor our middleware product, drawing on your in\-depth knowledge of networking and client/server dynamics

**Keys to Success:**

* Bachelor’s degree in biomedical engineering or a related field or equivalent experience.
* Proven experience in a scientific or engineering field (e.g., clinical/research laboratories, electronics, biotechnology, medical devices).
* Proven IT skills for laboratory middleware management.
* Strong technical competence and problem\-solving abilities
* Customer\-focused with excellent interpersonal skills.
* Proficiency in English and Italian – both written and verbal.
* Availability to travel at short notice and occasionally work weekends/unsociable hours (75 – 85% of working on field).
* Full driving license.

**What we offer:**

* A super team in which openness and team spirit are lived.
* A company car that can also be used privately.
* A job with an innovative employer.
* Outstanding development and career prospects in a globally growing company.
* A workplace culture in which integrity, involvement, intensity, and innovation are lived.


When your part of the team at Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll do important work, like helping customers in finding cures for cancer, protecting the environment, or making sure our food is safe. Your work will have real\-world impact, and you’ll be supported in achieving your career goals.  

  

As an employer, Thermo Fisher Scientific is committed to equal opportunity and cultural minority support and does not discriminate based on ethnic origin, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or legally protected status.


Join Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. today and be part of a diverse and inclusive community dedicated to making the world healthier, cleaner, and safer. As an equal opportunity employer, we value individuals with diverse experiences, backgrounds, and perspectives. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodation for individuals with disabilities throughout the recruitment process. Please contact us to request accommodation and start your journey with us.

title: Principal Biostatistician - FSP Europe
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.  

  

DESCRIPTION:  

Join our Clinical Research team as a Principal Scientist in Biostatistics, where you'll contribute to advancing life\-changing therapies through statistical excellence. As part of our Functional Service Provider (FSP) division, you'll work closely with clients while utilizing the benefits and resources of an established leader in clinical research. You'll be responsible for implementing diverse statistical tasks throughout the full clinical trial process, from study design and protocol writing to final analysis and reporting. This position offers the opportunity to impact health outcomes while working with advanced clinical development programs.  

  

REQUIREMENTS:  

* Ph. D or MS Biostatistics Degree plus minimum 10 years of experience in clinical research, with emphasis on Phase I\-III trials with compound Lead level experience
* Preferred Fields of Study: Statistics, Biostatistics, or related quantitative field
* Expert\-level proficiency in SAS programming and statistical methodology
* Strong knowledge of ICH\-GCP guidelines and clinical trial regulations
* Demonstrated experience in statistical analysis plan development and review
* Ability to manage multiple clinical trials simultaneously
* Advanced knowledge of CDISC standards and their implementation
* Experience with statistical software packages such as R, EAST, or WinBUGS
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills
* Excellence in written and verbal communication
* Ability to interact effectively with regulatory authorities (FDA, EMA)
* Experience in CRO environment and vendor management preferred
* Knowledge of auto\-immune and/or oncology therapeutic areas beneficial
* Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in teams
* Ability to meet deadlines and manage competing priorities
* Strong attention to detail and quality focus

title: mRNA Project Manager Intern
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. is the world leader in serving science, with annual revenue of approximately $40 billion. Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Whether our customers are accelerating life sciences research, solving complex analytical challenges, growing efficiency in their laboratories, improving patient health through diagnostics or the development and manufacture of life\-changing therapies, we are here to support them. Our global team of more than 100,000 colleagues delivers an unrivaled combination of innovative technologies, purchasing convenience and pharmaceutical services through our industry\-leading brands, including Thermo Scientific, Applied Biosystems, Invitrogen, Fisher Scientific, Unity Lab Services, Patheon and PPD.  

  

Our internship program will give you a chance to get real world business experience and build your network before graduation.


We are seeking an upbeat, responsible intern to join our growing organization. In this position, you will be expected to learn the ins\-and\-outs of our daily routines and procedures. You will focus on learning how our organization runs, and can be expected to gain valuable insight that can foster your interest in your chosen career field.  

You will join the Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Plant in Monza to support our new mRNA Department. Using your academic background, you will gain a hands\-on experience in a highly multifaceted and international manufacturing environment.


During the six\-month paid internship, you will focus on the following activities:  

* Collaborate with colleagues in supporting all the planning and the follow\-up activities related to the mRNA projects.
* Collaborate with colleagues in drafting and reviewing the documentation related to Project Manager (planning, risk tracker, contract change...).
* Support the colleagues for holding meeting and correspondence between internal and client team.

How you will get there:  

* MSc degree or equivalent (depending to country), in a scientific /pharmaceutical area (FCT, Pharmacy, Chemistry, Biotechnology) or engineering;
* English \+ Italian: Advanced written and oral communication skills;
* Computer skills: advanced MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint);
* Leadership, good communication skills, advanced interpersonal and analytical abilities.


Thermo Fisher Scientific is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, religion, color, national or ethnic origin, citizenship, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, genetic information, veteran status, age or disability status.
